headings.usage: Massive serif headlines for TV projects, dashboard page titles, and split screen messaging.

body.usage: Clean, highly readable UI text for forms, navigation, buttons, and descriptions.

data.usage: Numbers, currency, tables, data points, and metadata tags.

visual_enhancers.borders: Sharp 1px solid borders on all cards, tables, and inputs. No soft box-shadows. Rely on structural grayscale and high contrast.
visual_enhancers.radius: 0px or 2px maximum. Sharp edges reinforce the premium, executive, Bloomberg/Financial Times B2B feel.
visual_enhancers.spacing: Generous padding inside components (p-6, p-8) but tight grid gaps (gap-4) for data density.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle grain or noise on dark surfaces (like the login left panel or sidebar) to give an organic, premium feel rather than flat vectors.

components.cards: Flat white surfaces with 1px border (border-[#E4E4E1]). No shadows. Use borders to separate grid items.
components.buttons: Sharp rectangles. Primary buttons use Cobalt Blue (#0033A0). Secondary buttons use transparent background with 1px black border.
components.inputs: Shadcn inputs customized with 0px radius, sharp focus rings (2px Cobalt Blue), and IBM Plex Sans font.
components.tables: High density. IBM Plex Mono for numeric columns. 1px bottom border on rows, no vertical dividers.
components.sidebar: Fixed, dark executive navy (#0A1128) background. Object-based navigation. Light text.
components.charts: Recharts. Simple bars and lines. Minimal grid lines, no complex 3D charts.

layouts.login: 50/50 Split screen. Left: Immersive dark image overlay (black/70) with Playfair Display messaging about the network's vision. Right: Stone background (#F9F9F6), vertically centered minimal form (Email, Password, Sign In).
layouts.dashboard: 260px Dark Sidebar. Top header with breadcrumbs and user profile. Main content area structured in a strict grid.
layouts.tv_project_page: Editorial layout. Massive edge-to-edge hero image with dark gradient overlay. Content in a centered reading column (max-w-4xl) with a sticky right sidebar for Sponsorship Checkout.

login_background.description: Executive broadcast control room for the left side of the split login screen. Apply a heavy dark overlay.

tv_project_hero_1.description: Nature documentary landscape filming hero image for a TV project presentation.

tv_project_hero_2.description: Investigative journalism vintage desk for a documentary series presentation.

libraries.usage: Use react-simple-maps for the 'International banner campaign builder' to show a clean, 2D minimalist world map for country selection. Avoid 3D globe as it contradicts the flat, editorial aesthetic.

instructions_to_main_agent: NOTE: The project uses .js files, NOT .tsx files. Write all React code in standard Javascript + JSX.
Install Google Fonts in index.css: Playfair Display, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono.
Implement Shadcn UI but override ALL rounded corners to 0px or 2px. Remove default Vercel-like soft shadows in Tailwind config.
Build the Login page as a split screen at the root path ('/'). No registration links. It must feel like a closed, private portal.
Build an Admin Dashboard ('/admin') and Representative Dashboard ('/rep').
In the Representative Dashboard, implement the Banner Campaign Builder with a multi-select list of countries (use react-simple-maps if feasible, otherwise a well-styled list/grid of regions).
For TV Sponsorships, build an index page with an editorial grid, and a detail page that feels like a 'Financial Times' long-form article.
Use lucide-react for icons. Keep them sharp and minimal.
Use pure HTML/Tailwind for the high-end TV Project presentation pages, relying on standard Shadcn only for the checkout/form interactions.
DO NOT use complete `transparent` background in header, overlays, or any surface that has content under it.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and crisp 1px borders rather than soft shadows.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) only where necessary, avoiding muddy grey transparency.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file.
Always use sharp button styles with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, custom selection states, and loading animations separate good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
